name: Świderski 2008 single-stage validation
description: Reproduces J. Świderski, A. Zając, M. Skorczakowski, 'Diode-seeded nanosecond Yb-doped fiber amplifier operating at the repetition rate up to 500 kHz,' Optica Applicata XXXVIII(4), 669-676 (2008). Single-stage MOFPA: pulsed 1063.91 nm laser-diode seed, Nufern Yb-doped LMA double-clad gain fiber (20/400 µm, NA 0.06, 13 dB total absorption at 976 nm), 978 nm CW co-propagating pump (11.4 W launched into fiber). Seed enters via fiber-pigtailed isolator and free-space launch (~70% efficiency). The validation script overrides seed.average_power and seed.pulse_duration to sweep across the 100-kHz test points published in the paper's Fig. 5 / Table.

metadata.validation_target: Świderski et al., Optica Applicata XXXVIII(4), 669-676 (2008)
metadata.fiber_part: Nufern Yb-doped LMA double-clad, 20/400 µm, NA_core 0.06, NA_clad 0.46
metadata.clad_absorption_source: Paper states 13 dB total absorption at 976 nm (95% pump absorbed). Length 2.7 m × 4.8 dB/m = 12.96 dB total. The 4.8 dB/m at 976 nm is consistent with Coherent/Nufern published spec for their LMA-YDF-20/400-VIII fiber family (typically 4.6-5.4 dB/m at 976 nm); paper does not give the per-meter rate so this is a reasonable estimate. The actual fiber length is described as 'optimal length specified by Nufern for ~95% absorption' but the value is not numerically tabulated in the paper.
metadata.linewidth_source: Paper states 3-dB spectral width 0.52 nm at 1063.91 nm. Δν = c·Δλ/λ² ≈ 138 GHz.
metadata.launch_path: Seed pigtail has built-in isolator (PER >22 dB, IL not stated; assumed ~0.5 dB). Free-space coupling onto gain-fiber facet has ~70% launch efficiency (paper). Combined: -1.55 dB modeled here as a single Isolator component named PIGTAIL-ISO+LAUNCH.
metadata.pump_launch_efficiency: Paper states pump launching efficiency >90%. The 11.4 W value used here is the power *launched into* the active fiber per the paper's Fig. 4-6 axes ('Pump power launched into the fiber'), so no additional pump-side correction is applied.
metadata.validation_script: validation/run_validation.py runs the model at the published 100 kHz, 11.4 W pump test points (Table on page 671): seed avg powers 1.5/2.7/4.0/7.5 mW (after pigtail; this JSON's seed.average_power is set to 1.5 mW for the 11 ns case as a default, but the script overrides per test point) at pulse durations 11/30/50/100 ns, expecting outputs 2.04/2.44/2.6/2.97 W respectively (per Fig 5).
